_comment: Platt scaling per category: p = 1 / (1 + exp(A*s + B)) where s = mean token logprob of the local answer. Overwritten by eval/calibrate.py from labeled mock runs. Constants below are anchored to Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 measured on the CPU box (hard set, 2026-07-09): correct local answers scored s in [-0.21, -0.05]; this curve maps s=-0.10 -> 0.73, s=-0.15 -> 0.65, s=-0.20 -> 0.55, s=-0.30 -> 0.35, so clearly-confident answers pass theta while shaky ones still escalate. The previous A=-3/B=-0.5 defaults mapped even a PERFECT logprob (0.0) to 0.62 -- barely over theta -- rejecting near-certain answers and leaking remote tokens. theta = minimum calibrated confidence to accept a local answer without escalation.
